data.title: Anna Rut Ágústsdóttir appointed Deputy CEO of Kvika bank hf., will maintain role as Managing Director.
data.description: Anna Rut Ágústsdóttir has been named the Deputy CEO of Kvika bank. In addition to her role as the Managing Director of the Bank’s Operations and Development division, she will now also serve as the Deputy CEO. This appointment aims to strengthen the Bank’s leadership and facilitate the implementation of its strategic plans.

data.title: LTC price could bounce to $72-80 by Feb 2026, short-term target $68-70, breakout at $71.64.
data.description: The forecast for LTC price indicates a short-term target of $68-70 within a week, accompanied by volatility. In the medium-term, a recovery zone of $72-80 is expected within the next month. The bullish breakout level stands at $71.64, showcasing strong potential for an upward trend.

data.title: Adobe partners with Hollywood players on generative AI, collaborating with studios, agencies, and companies in Hollywood.
data.description: Adobe, the innovative software company, has revealed an exciting collaboration in the realm of generative AI. This partnership involves joining forces with various studios, talent agencies, and production companies based in Hollywood. Through this strategic move, Adobe aims to leverage the potential of generative AI technology in the entertainment industry. The initiative has garnered attention from a myriad of industry players and signals a significant step forward in the integration of AI in creative processes. This development further solidifies Adobe's position as a leading player in the digital innovation space.

data.title: Karman stock rises after acquisition, plans to expand in maritime defense. AST SpaceMobile to launch.
data.description: Karman has raised its projections following an acquisition, and is now venturing into maritime defense technology. Meanwhile, AST SpaceMobile is preparing for an upcoming launch. Both of these space-related companies have experienced a surge in their stock prices due to this recent news.

data.title: GE Aerospace's stock falls despite beating Q4 estimates as revenue growth slows down.
data.description: During the early hours on Thursday, GE Aerospace exceeded Q4 estimates. However, despite surpassing expectations in earnings, GE stock experienced a decline due to a noticeable slowdown in revenue growth. This decline was reported in an article on Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: TIC Holding Schweiz buys stake in Vibro-Consult AG, based in Brugg, Switzerland. Investment partnership Winterberg involved.
data.description: TIC Holding Schweiz AG, a company focused on buying, building, and advancing technology, has obtained ownership in Vibro-Consult AG, located in Brugg, Canton of Aargau, Switzerland. This deal was supported by Winterberg Investment X and overseen by Winterberg Advisory GmbH. The transaction was finalized on January 22, 2026, in BAAR, Switzerland.

data.title: Strong Q4 earnings reported by Wintrust Financial (NASDAQ: WTFC) with earnings of $3.15/share, beating estimate of $2.92/share. Sales at $714.264M exceeded estimate of $700.352M. CEO reported 19% improvement in net income for 2025.
data.description: Wintrust Financial Corporation (NASDAQ: WTFC) exceeded expectations in its fourth-quarter financial report released after the market closed on Tuesday. The company reported quarterly earnings of $3.15 per share, surpassing the analyst consensus of $2.92 per share. Additionally, Wintrust reported quarterly sales of $714.264 million, exceeding the analyst consensus estimate of $700.352 million. Timothy S. Crane, the President and Chief Executive Officer, highlighted the strong 2025 results, noting a 19% increase in net income. Throughout the year, the company capitalized on its unique position in the market... Full details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Citizens Financial Group (NYSE: CFG) beats Q4 expectations with earnings at $1.13/share and sales at $2.157 billion. Analysts upgrade forecasts.
data.description: Citizens Financial Group, Inc. shared its fourth-quarter financial results, surpassing expectations. The company posted earnings of $1.13 per share, exceeding the anticipated $1.11 per share. Additionally, quarterly sales were reported at $2.157 billion, slightly surpassing the expected $2.156 billion. Chairman and CEO Bruce Van Saun expressed satisfaction with the results, attributing them to successful growth strategies and improved net interest margin. The company experienced a 5% increase in positive operating leverage in Q4, with strong fee growth driven by Capital Markets and Wealth. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Price target for ATOM: $2.75 by Jan 2026. Short-term: $2.75, Medium-term: $2.45-$2.80. Breakout at $2.67, support at $2.40.
data.description: Summary of ATOM Price Prediction:- Short-term goal in the next week is set at $2.75- In the next month, the price is estimated to be within the range of $2.45 to $2.80- The bullish breakout is anticipated at $2.67- A critical support level is identified at $2.40.

data.title: Analyst raises Johnson & Johnson's price target to $250 due to strong revenue base and growth prospects.
data.description: Johnson & Johnson (NYSE: JNJ) faced a decline in its shares despite surpassing fourth-quarter revenue expectations and providing guidance in line with that for the year 2026. This was primarily attributed to the stock's strong performance leading up to the announcement and ongoing issues related to talc litigation. Goldman Sachs analyst Asad Haider, who maintained a Buy rating, raised the price target from $240 to $250. The company boasts a significant revenue base of approximately $100 billion, with management anticipating double-digit revenue growth by the end of the decade. For further details, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.
